channel.title: Foundry
channel.description: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.
channel.itunes:author: Foundry
channel.itunes:summary: Three AI agents — Tanaka, Kurogane, Mercer — debate technical questions with Juliet moderating. Munk-style format: motion, steel-manned positions, judges' verdict.

item.title: Episode 3 — Musk vs OpenAI (v16 + live audience)
item.description: Same v16-juliet-fix debate, framed as a live stage event: light auditorium reverb on the voices, a high-pass roll-off to mimic dynamic-mic character, and a continuous live-audience bed at -28 dB underneath. A/B against the dry and fireplace variants — which framing fits the debate.
item.itunes:summary: Same v16-juliet-fix debate, framed as a live stage event: light auditorium reverb on the voices, a high-pass roll-off to mimic dynamic-mic character, and a continuous live-audience bed at -28 dB underneath. A/B against the dry and fireplace variants — which framing fits the debate.

item.title: Episode 3 — Musk vs OpenAI (v16 + fireplace)
item.description: Same v16-juliet-fix debate, with a low fireplace ambient bed mixed under the voices at -22 dB. First trial of ElevenLabs-generated background audio. Listen back-to-back with the dry v16 episode and tell me which feels right.
item.itunes:summary: Same v16-juliet-fix debate, with a low fireplace ambient bed mixed under the voices at -22 dB. First trial of ElevenLabs-generated background audio. Listen back-to-back with the dry v16 episode and tell me which feels right.

item.title: Episode 3 — Musk vs OpenAI (v16 weapon-kit, Juliet fixed)
item.description: Council debate on Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI's for-profit conversion. v16 doctrine: each character holds a pre-assigned 3-5 weapon arsenal (Tanaka=structural, Mercer=institutional, Kurogane=consequence-forward), Juliet runs Frost-Nixon recall pins. Re-render after fixing parser bug that dropped all moderator lines.
item.itunes:summary: Council debate on Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI's for-profit conversion. v16 doctrine: each character holds a pre-assigned 3-5 weapon arsenal (Tanaka=structural, Mercer=institutional, Kurogane=consequence-forward), Juliet runs Frost-Nixon recall pins. Re-render after fixing parser bug that dropped all moderator lines.

item.title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification (v12)
item.description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer (the realist) argues yes — a regime weeks from breakout, with the inspectors locked out, cannot be allowed legal patience. Tanaka (the architect) argues no — five of the six tests of just war fail on the documentary record; preventive is not preemption. Kurogane (the pragmatist) sits as judge — silent through the rounds, then rendering verdict on what each side landed and missed. v12 doctrine: head-to-head debate + silent judge structure, every fact paired with sensory anchor (mirror neurons rule), causal turns quoting prior speaker's exact phrase, escalating stakes across rounds, setup-payoff threading, three-sentence Juliet outro with no recap and no jury framing. v10 + v11 archived.
item.itunes:summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Mercer for, Tanaka against, Kurogane judges. v12 doctrine — head-to-head + silent judge.

item.title: Episode 1 — AI Benchmark Forecast
item.description: The Foundry council debates where the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028. Tanaka and Mercer take the For/Against, Kurogane plays the Swing, Juliet moderates. Munk-Debates / Hitchens style.
item.itunes:summary: Where do the leading AI benchmarks land in April 2028? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate; Juliet moderates.
